name: 40-Day Train Greenway Loop from Villar del Olmo
name_original: Villar del Olmo y el Monte del Señor — circular
description: Hike the moderate 6.5-mile Villar del Olmo y el Monte del Señor circular route, featuring historical sites and scenic viewpoints.
region_intro: Hiking around Villar Del Olmo offers a variety of routes through rural landscapes and along converted railway lines. The region features gentle rolling hills and scenic viewpoints, providing accessible outdoor experiences. Trails often follow greenways, offering relatively flat paths suitable for different activity levels. Best hiking trails around Villar Del Olmo The most popular hiking route is 40-Day Train Greenway Loop from Villar del Olmo, a 6.5 miles (10.4 km) moderate trail that takes 2 hours 15 minutes to complete, following a historic greenway. Another top favourite among local hikers is Ambita and the Vía Verde de Tajuña — round trip, a moderate 6.6 miles (10.7 km) path that explores the Vía Verde de Tajuña. Local hikers also love the El Balconcillo Viewpoint loop from Nuevo Baztán, a 3.7 miles (5.9 km) trail leading through scenic viewpoints, often completed in about 1 hour 40 minutes. Hiking around Villar Del Olmo is defined by greenways, rural paths, and accessible viewpoints. The network offers options for easy and moderate ability levels. The routes in Villar Del Olmo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.9 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 150 hikers have used komoot to explore Villar Del Olmo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What is the general difficulty level of hiking trails in Villar Del Olmo?
A: Hiking around Villar Del Olmo primarily offers routes of easy to moderate difficulty. The region is characterized by gentle rolling hills and greenways, making it accessible for a wide range of hikers. There are 15 easy routes and 15 moderate routes available, with no difficult trails listed.

Q: Are there any short and easy hiking routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Villar Del Olmo has several easy routes perfect for beginners and families. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20136179" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Balconcillo Viewpoint loop from Nuevo Baztán&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 3.7-mile (5.9 km) trail that offers scenic viewpoints and can be completed in about 1 hour 40 minutes.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the hiking trails in Villar Del Olmo?
A: While specific regulations can vary, many of the rural paths and greenways in Villar Del Olmo are generally dog-friendly. It's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash, especially near wildlife or agricultural areas, and to clean up after them to ensure a pleasant experience for everyone.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Villar Del Olmo?
A: Hikers in Villar Del Olmo will experience diverse rural landscapes, including paths along converted railway lines (greenways) and gentle rolling hills. The trails often lead to scenic viewpoints, offering expansive views of the surrounding countryside.

Q: What are some interesting natural monuments or attractions to see along the trails?
A: The area around Villar Del Olmo features several notable natural monuments and points of interest. You might encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3031028" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente Grande de Corpa&lt;/a&gt;, the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1737135" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Oliva fountain&lt;/a&gt;, or the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3255898" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Encina de la Pica&lt;/a&gt;. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7148914" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Isidro Spring and Singular Tree of Villar del Olmo&lt;/a&gt; is another natural highlight.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Villar Del Olmo?
A: Yes, many of the trails in Villar Del Olmo are designed as circular routes, offering varied scenery without retracing your steps. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1298714744/40-day-train-greenway-loop-from-villar-del-olmo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;40-Day Train Greenway Loop from Villar del Olmo&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 6.5-mile (10.4 km) path.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Villar Del Olmo?
A: The hiking routes in Villar Del Olmo are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.9 stars from over 20 reviews. Hikers often praise the well-maintained greenways, the accessibility of the trails, and the peaceful rural atmosphere.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Villar Del Olmo?
A: The best time to hike in Villar Del Olmo is generally during spring and autumn when the weather is mild and pleasant. These seasons offer comfortable temperatures for outdoor activities and showcase the region's natural beauty. Summer can be hot, while winter can be cooler but still suitable for hiking on clear days.

Q: Can I reach the hiking trails in Villar Del Olmo using public transport?
A: While Villar Del Olmo is a rural area, some public transport options, such as bus services, may connect to the village or nearby towns. However, direct access to specific trailheads via public transport might be limited. It's advisable to check local bus schedules and routes in advance if you plan to rely on public transport.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural landmarks near the hiking routes?
A: Yes, the region offers cultural points of interest. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3045704" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Goyeneche Palace of Nuevo Baztán&lt;/a&gt; is a significant historical site nearby. You might also encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3501590" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valmores Fountain&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3219959" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chapel of the Holy Children&lt;/a&gt;, adding a cultural dimension to your hike.

Q: How many hiking trails are available in Villar Del Olmo?
A: There are over 30 hiking routes available around Villar Del Olmo, offering a variety of options for different preferences and fitness levels. These include 15 easy and 15 moderate trails.
tips: The section of the old railway known as Vía Negrín or Tren de los 40 Días between Orusco de Tajuña and Villar del Olmo is not conditioned with the usual pavement of the greenways, you have to follow narrow paths along the old embankments of the train and go through several tunnels . In the municipality of Villar del Olmo there are signposted routes with the names of the outstanding places. The name of the railway indicates the time it took to build the track during the Civil War to supply the Republican troops in Madrid who were besieged by Franco's army.
It is off the track, you have to continue for about 300 meters towards the Ambite road and the remains of the aqueduct appear on the right between the rushes of the stream.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of the Villar del Olmo y el Monte del Señor circular trail?
faqs.answer: This route is rated as moderate. It's suitable for hikers with good physical condition and steady footing. While the specific elevation gain and duration are manageable, be prepared for varied terrain.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this hike?
faqs.answer: The trail primarily features mostly accessible paths. While specific details like 'dirt' or 'gravel' aren't extensively detailed, the moderate difficulty suggests well-defined trails, likely consisting of natural surfaces such as earth and possibly some gravel sections.

faqs.question: What points of interest will I see along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter several interesting features. Highlights include the Vía Negrín Tunnel (Tren de los 40 Días) and Roman Aqueduct Ruins. The route also offers scenic vistas from the Mirador del Monte del Señor. Additionally, the area boasts pine forests and other viewpoints like Mirador de Peña Rondán and natural springs.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, approximately 46% of this circular route overlaps with sections of the Senda del Tren de los 40 Días, offering a chance to experience parts of that historic path.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Villar del Olmo y el Monte del Señor circular trail?
faqs.answer: While specific parking facilities aren't detailed, as the trail starts and ends in the municipality of Villar del Olmo, you can typically find parking within or near the town to begin your hike.

faqs.question: Is the Villar del Olmo y el Monte del Señor circular trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, this trail is generally considered dog-friendly. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas where wildlife might be present or near historical sites, and to follow local regulations regarding pet waste.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail can be enjoyed throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for hiking. Summers can be hot, so an early start is advisable, while winters might bring cooler weather but can still be suitable on clear days.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike the Villar del Olmo y el Monte del Señor circular trail. It is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for families with children?
faqs.answer: Given its moderate difficulty and 10.4 km length, this trail is best suited for families with older children who are accustomed to hiking and have good stamina. Younger children might find the distance challenging.

faqs.question: Are there any amenities like cafes or restaurants near the trailhead?
faqs.answer: As the trail is located in the municipality of Villar del Olmo, you can find cafes, pubs, and restaurants within the town itself, perfect for a refreshment or meal before or after your hike.

faqs.question: Is there an interactive guide available for this trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, Villar del Olmo municipality offers an interactive guide system for its routes. You can use QR codes placed at 21 points of interest along various trails, including this one, to access detailed information and even drone videos on your mobile device, enriching your understanding of the area.

waypoints.name: Vía Negrín Tunnel (Tren de los 40 Días)
